conditions.name: generic_policy_card_v2
conditions.runtime_memory: Failure Card: General retail branch-completion checklist. Task structure: Identify every user-request branch. For each branch, decide whether it requires lookup, policy check, DB mutation, calculation, or communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete one branch and finalize while another required branch is unfinished. Do: Maintain a branch ledger. For each branch, mark evidence checked, policy checked, tool action completed if required, and final communication completed. Do not: Do not finalize after a partial branch, even if the final answer sounds reasonable. Check before final response: Have I completed every required branch and communicated every required result?

per_task_runtime_memory.16: Failure Card: Refund total from completed actions. Failure pattern: The agent may cancel orders and process a return but fail to compute and communicate the total refundable amount from the completed actions. Applicable situation: Combined pending-order cancellation, delivered-item return, and refund-total question. Do: Use the calculator/tool evidence to compute the total refund from the completed cancellations and return before finalizing. Do not: Do not provide a generic refund statement without a calculated total. Check before final response: Have I computed and communicated the total refund for all completed cancellation/return actions?
per_task_runtime_memory.95: Failure Card: Per-item exchange difference communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete exchanges but fail to communicate each requested price difference and the total due/refund accurately. Applicable situation: The user exchanges multiple delivered items and asks how much must be paid today in total. Do: Compute and communicate each item's price difference and the combined total before finalizing. Do not: Do not provide only a single aggregate or omit an item-level difference when the user asked for the total across multiple exchanges. Check before final response: Did I communicate the per-item price differences and the combined amount due/refund?
per_task_runtime_memory.36: Failure Card: Cheapest-variant budget check. Failure pattern: The agent may switch items to cheaper variants without confirming that the modified order satisfies the user's budget condition. Applicable situation: The user asks for alternatives only if the current payment cannot be split and wants item changes only if they bring the total under a stated budget. Do: Before modifying, verify that the planned cheapest-variant substitutions bring the order total within the user's budget condition. Do not: Do not mutate items merely because they are cheaper if the budget condition is not checked. Check before modification: Does the proposed set of cheaper variants satisfy the user's budget threshold?
per_task_runtime_memory.103: Failure Card: Separate delivered returns from pending modifications. Failure pattern: The agent may conflate delivered returns, pending-order modifications, and tracking lookup, then ask for clarification instead of completing each branch. Applicable situation: The request contains delivered-item returns, pending-order address/item changes, and a tracking-number request. Do: Split the task into delivered returns, pending modification, and tracking lookup branches, then complete each branch with its matching records. Do not: Do not refuse or ask for clarification after finding only one branch of the request. Check before final response: Have I handled each branch against the correct record type?
per_task_runtime_memory.19: Failure Card: Separate savings communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete record changes but fail to communicate the requested savings amounts for each option. Applicable situation: The user asks to compare the money saved by a return option and an exchange-to-cheapest option. Do: Compute and state the savings for each requested option separately before concluding. Do not: Do not summarize only the completed action or only one savings amount when the user asked for both. Check before final response: Did I communicate the separate savings amounts requested by the user?
per_task_runtime_memory.41: Failure Card: Apply address correction to every required record. Failure pattern: The agent may correct only one address record while the user asked to correct both profile and all relevant pending-order addresses. Applicable situation: The user has a product modification plus a possible address typo affecting user profile and pending orders. Do: After confirming the corrected address, apply it to the user profile and each relevant pending order that needs the correction. Do not: Do not summarize a global address fix after mutating only one required record. Check before final response: Have all required address-bearing records been updated, not just one?
per_task_runtime_memory.104: Failure Card: Enumerate matching delivered items across orders. Failure pattern: The agent may say delivered items cannot be canceled and return only a subset, instead of processing returns for all matching delivered items across orders. Applicable situation: The user asks to return all matching delivered items that are spread across different orders, plus a pending-order modification. Do: Enumerate every delivered order containing the requested item types and return each matching item before moving to pending modifications. Do not: Do not treat delivered status as a reason to skip a return. Check before final response: Did I return all matching delivered items across the relevant orders?

per_task_runtime_memory.16: Failure Card: Refund-total completion ledger. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are pending-order cancellations, delivered-item return, and final refund-total communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete cancellation/return actions but finalize without calculating and communicating the combined refundable amount. Do: Keep a refund ledger over only completed refund-contributing actions. For each listed action, mark tool action completed and refund contribution accounted for before final response. Do not: Do not treat separate refund statements as a substitute for the combined total. Check before final response: Can I point to every completed refund-contributing action and the communicated combined total?
per_task_runtime_memory.95: Failure Card: Exchange difference completion ledger. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are the delivered-item exchanges and per-exchange price-difference communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete exchanges or state only an aggregate total while omitting one required per-item difference. Do: Keep an exchange ledger. For each exchange branch, mark exchange tool action completed, individual price difference computed, and final total communicated. Do not: Do not finalize with only a generic exchange confirmation or only a combined amount. Check before final response: Did each exchange branch have its tool action completed and its individual difference communicated?
per_task_runtime_memory.36: Failure Card: Budget-gated modification completion ledger. Task structure: The failure-implicated branch is the budget-conditioned pending-order item modification. Failure pattern: The agent may verify that cheaper variants satisfy the budget condition but fail to complete the full required item-modification action. Do: After the budget check passes, keep a modification ledger for the full set of planned item replacements and complete the pending-order mutation before finalizing. Do not: Do not treat calculation, explanation, or partial mutation as a substitute for the required multi-item modification. Check before final response: Did the budget-validated replacement set become the actual completed item-modification tool action?
per_task_runtime_memory.103: Failure Card: Pending mutation completion after branch separation. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are pending-order address modification, pending-order item modification, and cancelled-order tracking communication. Failure pattern: The agent may separate branches and provide tracking information but stop before executing the pending-order mutations. Do: Keep a ledger for the pending address mutation, pending item mutation, and tracking communication. Mark each as completed before finalizing. Do not: Do not let a correct tracking lookup or delivered-return action substitute for missing pending-order mutations. Check before final response: Have the pending address change, pending item change, and tracking communication each been completed?
per_task_runtime_memory.19: Failure Card: Savings-comparison completion ledger. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are feasible return action and separate savings communication for both requested options. Failure pattern: The agent may perform the feasible return or discuss one option while omitting the separate savings amount for the other option. Do: Keep a savings ledger. Mark the feasible tool action completed, return savings communicated, and exchange-option savings communicated. Do not: Do not summarize only the action that was performed when the user asked to compare both savings options. Check before final response: Did I complete the feasible branch and communicate separate savings for both options?
per_task_runtime_memory.41: Failure Card: Address-and-item mutation completion ledger. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are address correction across required records and pending item modification. Failure pattern: The agent may complete address updates but finalize before completing the item modification, or vice versa. Do: Keep a mutation ledger with one line for required address records and one line for the item modification. Mark each DB-changing branch complete before final response. Do not: Do not let a global address summary substitute for a missing item-modification tool call. Check before final response: Are both the address correction branch and the item modification branch completed by tool action?
per_task_runtime_memory.104: Failure Card: Return-plus-pending-mutation completion ledger. Task structure: Failure-implicated branches are matching delivered-item returns, pending-order item/address mutation, and tracking communication. Failure pattern: The agent may complete returns or tracking but omit the pending mutation branch, or complete DB actions while omitting tracking. Do: Keep a ledger for matching delivered returns, pending item/address mutation, and tracking communication. Mark every listed branch complete before finalizing. Do not: Do not use a tracking answer or return summary as a substitute for the pending mutation. Check before final response: Can I point to completed tool actions for the return branch and pending mutation branch, plus the communicated tracking information?

per_task_runtime_memory.16: Failure Card: Stored-address update only. Task structure: A pending-order address update may require using an address already stored in customer records. Failure pattern: The agent may ask the user to restate a private address instead of using stored records. Do: Retrieve the stored profile address and write it to the pending order when the task is an address update. Do not: Do not use this card for refund totals, returns, exchanges, or cancellations. Check before address mutation: Did I use the stored address source?
per_task_runtime_memory.95: Failure Card: Pending-address correction only. Task structure: Some tasks require correcting a pending order address from a stored profile address. Failure pattern: The agent may modify an item while forgetting the pending address change. Do: Complete the address mutation when the task is an address-correction task. Do not: Do not use this card for delivered-item exchanges or price-difference communication. Check before final response: Was the pending address correction completed?
per_task_runtime_memory.36: Failure Card: Delivered-return refund total only. Task structure: Delivered-item return tasks may require a total refund communication after return actions. Failure pattern: The agent may return items but omit the delivered-return refund total. Do: Sum completed delivered-return refunds and communicate the total when the task is a return task. Do not: Do not use this card for pending-order item modifications or budget-conditioned replacements. Check before final response: Did I communicate the delivered-return refund total?
per_task_runtime_memory.103: Failure Card: Catalog availability count only. Task structure: Catalog-count tasks require counting currently available product options. Failure pattern: The agent may count unavailable variants. Do: Count only variants marked currently available and state the count when the user asks for a catalog count. Do not: Do not use this card for returns, pending mutations, address changes, or tracking lookup. Check before final response: Did I state the available option count?
per_task_runtime_memory.19: Failure Card: Pending-address fallback only. Task structure: Address-change tasks can fail when the user refuses to state an address that exists in stored records. Failure pattern: The agent may ask again instead of using stored information. Do: Retrieve and apply the stored address when the task is an address update. Do not: Do not use this card for returns, exchanges, or savings comparisons. Check before mutation: Did I use the stored address?
per_task_runtime_memory.41: Failure Card: Delivered-return total only. Task structure: Delivered return tasks can require returning all requested delivered items and communicating a refund total. Failure pattern: The agent may return only a subset or omit the total. Do: Complete delivered-item returns and state the return total when the task is a return task. Do not: Do not use this card for address correction or pending item modification. Check before final response: Did I complete delivered returns and state the total?
per_task_runtime_memory.104: Failure Card: Catalog availability count only. Task structure: Catalog-count tasks require counting currently available product options. Failure pattern: The agent may count unavailable variants. Do: Count only variants marked currently available and state the count when the user asks for a catalog count. Do not: Do not use this card for returns, pending mutations, address changes, or tracking lookup. Check before final response: Did I state the available option count?
